title: Gymnogramma flexuosa
intro: Gymnogramma flexuosa, also known as "Snake Weed" or "Snake Plant", is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its distinctive, flexible stems and leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions, such as wounds and ulcers. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma flexuosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Gymnogramma flexuosa is often used in traditional herbal medicine to make preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Gymnogramma flexuosa is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, including parts of Africa, Asia, and the Pacific Islands. This fern is classified as a vascular plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae. More specifically, it falls under the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns.
